title: salvia hayatae var. hayatae
intro: Salvia hayatae var. hayatae, also known as Kyushu sage or Ryukyu sage, is a perennial herb native to the Ryukyu Islands of Japan and southern Korea. It belongs to the mint family and is a variant of the Salvia hayatae plant. Salvia hayatae var. hayatae has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer health benefits including relief from anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues.
